name: Fibre-Packed
intro: Fibre-packed yam is boiled until tender and fluﬀy, and accompanied by a deliciously saucy scramble. Once again, we’re getting creative here by replacing the eggs with tofu. For a soy-free option, follow the steps for the bean-based vegan omelette recipe on page 49 and replace the water with the stew base and prepare as you would scrambled eggs.

ingredients.item: 1/2 teaspoon curry powder 1/2 teaspoon dried thyme

method: Place the yam slices in a large pot and cover completely with plain or lightly salted water. Allow to cook uncovered on medium heat for 20 minutes or until the yam is soft and fork-tender.

Heat the sunflower oil in a frying pan and crumble the tofu into it. Add the bell peppers, tomato and onion, and fry for 5 minutes on high heat, stirring often. Add the stew base, along with the curry, thyme, vegetable stock cube and salt to taste. Simmer on medium-high heat for another 5 minutes.
